data.name: errors.not-found
data.comment: Title of the HTTP 404 ProblemDetails response. Short noun phrase; appears as the page heading on Bridge's /not-found page and as the JSON title field on REST 404 responses.

data.name: errors.not-found.detail
data.value: The requested resource could not be located.
data.comment: Body text on the HTTP 404 ProblemDetails response. Full sentence; AT reads it after the title. Avoid jargon — generic users see this when a tenant-subdomain route 404s.

data.name: errors.unauthorized
data.comment: Title shown when a user without an active session hits a tenant route. Keep imperative; pairs with a "Sign in" call-to-action button.

data.name: errors.unauthorized.detail
data.value: Your session has expired or you are not signed in.
data.comment: Body text on the HTTP 401 ProblemDetails response. Mentions both first-time-not-signed-in and session-expired conditions so users in either state get a useful explanation.

data.name: errors.forbidden
data.comment: Title for HTTP 403. Indicates the user is signed in but lacks permission for this resource. Generic enough to use across blocks (TenantAdmin, BusinessCases, etc.).

data.name: errors.forbidden.detail
data.value: You do not have permission to perform this action.
data.comment: Body text on HTTP 403. Avoid suggesting "contact your administrator" — Sunfish hosts both self-service tenants and managed-service customers; "permission" is universal.

data.name: errors.conflict
data.comment: Title for HTTP 409. Surfaces in the Bridge browser-shell when a CRDT merge conflict requires user attention. NOT a SyncState.Conflict (that's a UI indicator); this is a wire-protocol-level conflict response.

data.name: errors.conflict.detail
data.value: The request conflicts with the current state of the resource. Please review and try again.
data.comment: Body text on HTTP 409. Tells the user the request lost a race; encourages retry-with-rebase.

data.name: validation.required
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a required input is left empty. Generic; appears under any required field that's blank. Subject ("This field") refers to the labelled input above.

data.name: validation.min-length
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when input is shorter than the minimum. {min} is an integer count of characters (not bytes / not graphemes). Used for text inputs and textareas.

data.name: validation.max-length
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when input exceeds the maximum length. {max} is an integer character count. Phrased as upper bound ("or fewer") — translators may flip to a culturally natural form.

data.name: validation.email-format
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a value fails RFC 5322 email parsing. "Email address" is the noun phrase; some locales prefer "email" alone — choose what's natural.

data.name: validation.url-format
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a value isn't a parseable absolute URL (http/https). "URL" is widely accepted as a loanword; do NOT translate as "web address" unless your locale strongly prefers it.

data.name: validation.numeric-only
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a numeric-only field receives non-digit characters. Covers phone-number digit-fields, postal codes, ID numbers — any field rejecting letters/punctuation.

data.name: validation.date-format
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a date input cannot be parsed. The locale's CultureInfo.DateTimeFormat governs accepted formats — translators may add a hint like "(YYYY-MM-DD)" if locale convention differs strongly from ISO.

data.name: validation.future-date-only
data.value: Date must be in the future.
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a date field requires a future date (e.g., scheduled appointments, deadlines). "Future" = strictly after today in the user's local time zone.

data.name: validation.past-date-only
data.value: Date must be in the past.
data.comment: Inline form-validation message when a date field requires a past date (e.g., birthdates, historical events). "Past" = strictly before today in the user's local time zone.

data.name: actions.save
data.comment: Button label — commit the current form/draft to the local node ledger. Primary action verb. Often paired with actions.cancel as the secondary.

data.name: actions.cancel
data.comment: Button label — discard the current draft and close the dialog/form. Secondary action verb. Pairs with actions.save (most common) or actions.delete (in confirmation dialogs).

data.name: actions.retry
data.comment: Button label — reattempt a failed network/sync operation. Appears in error toasts and in the SyncState=Failed indicator. Imperative verb, not noun.

data.name: actions.delete
data.comment: Button label — destructive action; removes a record. Almost always followed by actions.confirm in a modal. Some locales (de-DE, ja-JP) may prefer "Remove"-equivalent for non-destructive contexts — favor stronger word here since this IS destructive.

data.name: actions.confirm
data.comment: Button label — proceed with a previously-displayed action (typically destructive). Appears in confirmation dialogs as the affirmative. Imperative verb.

data.name: actions.edit
data.comment: Button label or menu item — switch a record from view-mode to edit-mode. Imperative verb. Common adjacent to inline-display fields.

data.name: actions.search
data.comment: Button label or input placeholder — execute a query. Imperative verb when a button; some locales (ja-JP, zh-CN) may render as a noun depending on context — the same key serves both.

data.name: status.loading
data.comment: Status label — initial fetch in progress. Often appears with a spinner; some UIs append an ellipsis client-side ("Loading...") so do NOT include the ellipsis in the value.

data.name: status.saving
data.comment: Status label — write-to-ledger in progress (after user pressed actions.save). Distinct from "loading" (read) — the verb tense matters for screen readers.

data.name: status.processing
data.comment: Status label — generic in-flight indicator for operations that aren't read or write (sync, export, migration, computation). Used when more specific status verbs don't apply.

data.name: status.completed
data.comment: Status label — terminal success state. Past participle. Pairs with a check-mark icon in step-trackers.

data.name: status.failed
data.comment: Status label — terminal failure state. Past participle. Pairs with an error icon and typically with actions.retry as the recovery affordance.

data.name: status.idle
data.comment: Status label — no operation in flight; ready for input. Default state of the SyncState indicator when fully synced and quiescent. Some locales may prefer "Ready" — use the locale's natural quiescent-state word.
